jsonLdWeb.name: Mortar Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate mortar bags, sand, and water for brick and block masonry projects.

mainEntity.name: How much mortar do I need per square foot of brick wall?

acceptedAnswer.text: For standard modular bricks with 3/8" joints, one 80 lb bag of mortar covers about 35 square feet of wall. With 1/2" joints, coverage drops to about 25 square feet per bag. The exact amount depends on brick size, joint width, and mortar consistency.

mainEntity.name: How much sand do I need for mortar?

acceptedAnswer.text: For premixed mortar (Type S or N bags), no additional sand is needed — it is already included. For site-mixed mortar using Portland cement and lime, you need about 3 parts sand to 1 part cite. Per 80 lb bag of mortar mix, expect about 0.5 cubic feet of total volume.

mainEntity.name: How much water per bag of mortar?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use approximately 5-6 quarts (1.25-1.5 gallons) of water per 80 lb bag of premixed mortar. Add water gradually and mix until mortar reaches a workable, butter-like consistency. Too much water weakens the mortar.

mainEntity.name: What type of mortar should I use?

acceptedAnswer.text: Type S mortar for structural, below-grade, and retaining walls (2,300 psi). Type N for above-grade, non-load-bearing walls and veneer (750 psi). Type M for heavy loads and below-grade foundations (2,500 psi). Type O for interior non-load-bearing only (350 psi).

mainEntity.name: How long does mortar stay workable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mortar remains workable for about 1.5 to 2 hours after mixing in normal conditions. In hot weather (above 90°F) it may set in under 1 hour. Mix only as much as you can use in 90 minutes. Do not re-temper mortar after it begins to set.

mainEntity.name: How much mortar for CMU blocks?

acceptedAnswer.text: For standard 8×8×16 CMU blocks with 3/8" joints, one 80 lb bag covers about 12-14 blocks (approximately 12 sq ft of wall). Blocks use less mortar per sq ft than bricks because the units are larger with fewer joints.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Mortar

Mortar calculations are based on wall area divided by the coverage per bag for your unit type and joint thickness.

Bags = Wall Area (sq ft) ÷ Coverage per Bag (sq ft)
  • Standard brick, 3/8" joint: ~35 sq ft per 80 lb bag
  • Standard brick, 1/2" joint: ~25 sq ft per bag
  • CMU block, 3/8" joint: ~12 sq ft per bag
  • Sand: ~0.5 cu ft per bag (for site-mixed mortar)
  • Water: ~1.25 gallons per 80 lb bag
  • Add 10% for waste, drops, and repointing
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How much mortar per square foot of brick?

About 1 bag per 35 sq ft with standard bricks and 3/8" joints. With 1/2" joints, plan 1 bag per 25 sq ft. Coverage varies with brick size and mortar consistency.

How much sand for mortar?

Premixed bags need no extra sand. For site-mixed mortar, use 3 parts sand to 1 part cement. Plan about 0.5 cu ft of sand per 80 lb bag equivalent.

How much water per bag?

About 1.25-1.5 gallons per 80 lb bag. Add gradually until you reach a butter-like consistency. Too much water weakens the mortar.

What mortar type should I use?

Type S for structural and below-grade walls (2,300 psi). Type N for non-load-bearing above-grade walls (750 psi). Type M for heavy loads and foundations.

How long is mortar workable?

About 1.5-2 hours in normal conditions. Under 1 hour in hot weather above 90°F. Mix only what you can use in 90 minutes.

How much mortar for CMU blocks?

One 80 lb bag covers 12-14 standard 8×8×16 blocks (about 12 sq ft of wall). Blocks require less mortar per sq ft than bricks due to fewer joints.
